reasoning: The question asks whether Ramzan Kadyrov will cease to be the Head of the Chechen Republic before June 1, 2025. Today is May 12, 2025, which gives a timeframe of less than 3 weeks (19 days). On May 5-6, 2025, Kadyrov made remarks about asking Putin to relieve him of his post, which briefly fueled rumors of an imminent departure. These remarks coincided with reports of his deteriorating health (pancreatic necrosis) and speculation about a power transition. However, Kadyrov quickly walked back these comments, stating he is a 'foot soldier' and that Putin decides his fate. Crucially, Kadyrov traveled to Moscow and met with Putin on May 7, 2025. This meeting served to silence the resignation rumors and reaffirm his position; multiple sources indicate Putin rejected the request (whether it was genuine or merely a tactic to negotiate for resources and security guarantees). The history of Kadyrov making identical 'resignation' claims 3 to 4 times over the last decade without stepping down strongly suggests this is his standard political theater. With the May 7 meeting resolving the immediate crisis and keeping him in power, the only realistically remaining pathways to him stepping down *within the next 19 days* would be an sudden death/incapacitation from his illness or an abrupt assassination/coup. While his illness is severe, his ability to travel to Moscow suggests he is not within days of death. Therefore, the probability of him ceasing to be head of Chechnya before June 1, 2025, is extremely low.
